title: Phone number country codes truncated to 2 digits during Salesforce nightly import since ~2 weeks ago
problem: Customer phone numbers with country codes longer than 2 digits are being truncated during the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow import. For example, Ireland's +353 becomes +35, Antigua's +1268 becomes +12. US (+1) and UK (+44) numbers are unaffected because their country codes are already ≤2 digits. Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are affected, and the corrupted data persists in storage (confirmed via export).
root_cause_hypothesis: A code change made approximately 2 weeks ago to improve international phone number format handling likely introduced a hard truncation of the country code field to 2 digits (or parses/slices the country code incorrectly). The import pipeline applies this faulty parsing when writing Salesforce data into TaskFlow's database, corrupting numbers with 3+ digit country codes at write time.
reproduction_steps: Identify a customer record in Salesforce with a country code longer than 2 digits (e.g., Ireland +353, India +91 should be fine but +91x would not be)
Wait for or manually trigger the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow import sync
Compare the phone number in TaskFlow to the source value in Salesforce
Observe that the country code is truncated to 2 digits in TaskFlow
environment: TaskFlow production environment with nightly Salesforce CRM import job. Issue began approximately 2 weeks ago following a code change to international phone number handling.
impact: Support team cannot reach ~50-100 international customers by phone. Data integrity is compromised for all records with country codes longer than 2 digits. The corruption is ongoing — each nightly import re-corrupts any manually corrected records. Domestic (US) and UK customers are unaffected.
recommended_fix: 1. Search git history from the past 2-3 weeks for changes to phone number parsing/formatting/import logic. 2. Review how country codes are extracted or validated — look for a hard-coded length limit of 2, a substring/slice operation like `code[:2]`, or a regex that captures only 2 digits for the country code. 3. Fix the parsing to support variable-length country codes (1-3 digits per E.164). 4. After deploying the fix, run a one-time re-sync from Salesforce to repair the ~50-100 corrupted records. Verify by spot-checking Ireland (+353) and other 3-digit country code records.
proposed_test_case: Unit test: pass phone numbers with 1-digit (+1), 2-digit (+44), and 3-digit (+353) country codes through the import parsing function and assert the full number is preserved. Integration test: simulate a Salesforce import payload containing an Irish number (+353 1 234 5678) and verify the stored value in TaskFlow's database matches exactly.
information_gaps: Exact commit or PR that changed phone number handling ~2 weeks ago (dev team can find via git log)
Whether the corruption affects only the import path or also manual entry and API writes

Complete list of affected country codes beyond Ireland (+353) and Antigua (+1268) — a DB query filtering for records with country codes ≤2 digits where the source country should have 3 digits would enumerate these
